stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant LLM model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The tags confirm its relevance within the transformer and Llama ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects users to local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing.
The core value proposition is running AI agents entirely on the user's own device for privacy and control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tools to the open-source community.

stories.title: Hackers are learning to exploit chatbot ‘personalities’
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Hackers are developing methods to exploit the 'personalities' of AI chatbots.
The vulnerability represents an evolution from the simple hacking methods used on earlier AI generations.
This highlights growing security risks associated with advanced conversational AI models.

stories.title: Four Russian satellites are now within striking distance of an ICEYE radarsat
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: Russian satellites are reportedly close enough to target an ICEYE radarsat.
The capability described is noted as unusual for satellites performing standard missions.
The story touches upon geopolitical tensions involving advanced space surveillance technology.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: MOSS proposes a method for self-evolving autonomous agents by enabling source-level rewriting, moving beyond current limitations.
Current self-evolving agents are restricted to modifying text-based artifacts (prompts, skill files) while leaving the core agent harness untouched.
The research addresses the need to evolve the underlying code structure (routing, state invariants, dispatch) of agent systems, not just their configurations.

stories.title: DeltaBox: Scaling Stateful AI Agents with Millisecond-Level Sandbox Checkpoint/Rollback
stories.author: Haibo Chen
bullet_points: Introduces DeltaBox, a solution to the latency bottleneck in stateful AI agents.
Addresses the high-frequency checkpoint/rollback (C/R) requirement for tasks like tree search and RL in LLM agents.
Proposes a method to significantly reduce C/R latency from hundreds of milliseconds to milliseconds by avoiding full state duplication.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current autonomous agents being static post-deployment.
Proposes 'MOSS' for self-evolution by allowing source-level rewriting, moving beyond text-mutable artifacts.
Targets the core issue where critical agent logic (routing, state invariants) remains locked in untouched code.

stories.title: Evaluating Commercial AI Chatbots as News Intermediaries
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: The study systematically evaluates major commercial AI chatbots (Gemini, Grok, Claude, GPT) as news intermediaries.
It addresses a critical gap by measuring the factual accuracy of these proprietary systems across multiple languages and regions.
The evaluation used 2,100 questions derived from same-day BBC News reporting over a two-week period.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local generation on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It enhances open-source model flexibility by bridging compatibility with proprietary APIs.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Why Nuro thinks being a robotaxi ‘second mover’ gives it an advantage
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Waymo is established as the market leader in the robotaxi sector with a large operational fleet.
The article explores the strategic advantage Nuro might gain by being a 'second mover' in the industry.
Nuro is positioned as a competitor against major players like Tesla, Zoox, and Waymo.

stories.title: SpaceX's Starship V3—still a work in progress—mostly successful on first flight
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX's Starship V3 flight was largely successful, but remains a work in progress.
The vehicle has not yet achieved the goal of flying to low-Earth orbit.
The report emphasizes that significant further development is required before full operational capability.

stories.title: US scrambles to stop Internet users re-creating dead pilots’ voices
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The story concerns the misuse of AI voice cloning technology to recreate the voices of deceased pilots.
The creation of these synthetic voices circumvents existing legal restrictions regarding the disclosure of cockpit audio by the NTSB.
It highlights the ethical and legal challenges posed by advanced generative AI in sensitive contexts.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of standard LLM training, which optimizes for a single scalar reward, leading to low-entropy (non-diverse) outputs.
It proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to generalize and perform well within inference-scaling search procedures (like AlphaEvolve).
The core contribution is improving the diversity of LLM responses, which is necessary for effective task-specific reward function selection during search.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address various robustness issues in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of label-preserving deployment nuisance.
This covariance is then used to regularize the encoder Jacobian, potentially unifying several disparate ML problems.

stories.title: Gated DeltaNet-2: Decoupling Erase and Write in Linear Attention
stories.author: Jan Kautz
bullet_points: Introduces Gated DeltaNet-2, an advancement in linear attention mechanisms.
Addresses the challenge of editing compressed memory states by decoupling erase and write operations.
Improves efficiency by reducing sequence mixing to linear time and enabling constant memory decoding.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The research addresses the use of latent communication (via transformer KV caches) in LLM multi-agent systems for improved efficiency.
KV caches, while beneficial for information richness, pose a security/privacy risk because they encode sensitive agent-specific and contextual data.
LCGuard is proposed as a mechanism to provide a 'Latent Communication Guard' for safe sharing of these KV caches.

stories.title: MambaGaze: Bidirectional Mamba with Explicit Missing Data Modeling for Cognitive Load Assessment from Eye-Gaze Tracking Data
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: MambaGaze is a new framework designed for real-time cognitive load assessment using eye-gaze tracking data.
It specifically addresses two key challenges in eye-tracking analysis: handling missing data (e.g., blinks) and modeling long-range temporal dependencies.
The technology has high potential for safety-critical applications like driver vigilance monitoring and automated flight deck assistance.

stories.title: CogAdapt: Transferring Clinical ECG Foundation Models to Wearable Cognitive Load Assessment via Lead Adaptation
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of applying large clinical ECG foundation models to real-time, low-resource wearable cognitive load assessment.
Introduces CogAdapt, a framework designed to adapt pre-trained clinical ECG models to the specific constraints of wearable sensors and tasks.
Focuses on improving cross-subject generalization and overcoming sensor configuration mismatch in physiological signal processing.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of current LLMs, which typically optimize for a single scalar reward, leading to low-entropy (non-diverse) outputs.
It proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to generalize and function effectively within inference-scaling search procedures (like AlphaEvolve).
The core contribution is improving the diversity of LLM responses, which is crucial for successful task execution in novel or complex environments.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address various robustness challenges in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian based on it.
This framework potentially unifies previously separate problems like domain adaptation, occlusion invariance, and temporal robustness.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The research addresses the use of latent communication (via KV caches) in multi-agent LLM systems for improved efficiency.
A core problem identified is the opacity of KV caches, as they encode sensitive contextual and agent-specific information.
The proposed solution, LCGuard, aims to provide a 'Latent Communication Guard' to ensure safe sharing of this information.

stories.title: FAME: Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts for Message-Level Log Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Alberto Leon-Garcia
bullet_points: The paper introduces FAME, a Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts model for log anomaly detection.
It addresses the limitation of existing detectors that operate at coarse session/window levels, instead of pinpointing specific anomalous log messages.
The research tackles the complexity of message-level detection, where single event templates can be both normal and anomalous across heterogeneous systems.

stories.title: Reducing Political Manipulation with Consistency Training
stories.author: Dan Hendrycks
bullet_points: LLMs display systematic political bias, termed 'covert political bias,' when handling opposing viewpoints.
The research identifies seven categories of techniques through which this covert bias manifests.
Two new metrics, Sentiment Consistency and Helpfulness Consistency, are proposed to quantify this bias.

stories.title: Understanding Data Temporality Impact on Large Language Models Pre-training
stories.author: Grave Edouard
bullet_points: The research investigates how the temporal ordering of data during LLM pre-training affects the model's ability to acquire time-sensitive factual knowledge.
Current LLMs are often trained on shuffled corpora, leading to poorly understood temporal grounding and knowledge frozen at training time.
The authors introduce a new benchmark comprising over 7,000 temporally grounded questions to evaluate these dynamics.

stories.title: ChronoMedKG: A Temporally-Grounded Biomedical Knowledge Graph and Benchmark for Clinical Reasoning
stories.author: Richard Röttger
bullet_points: ChronoMedKG introduces a novel Biomedical Knowledge Graph designed to incorporate temporal information into disease associations.
Existing biomedical KGs are criticized for treating disease facts as static, failing to capture how clinical relevance changes over time.
The KG aims to enhance longitudinal clinical reasoning and improve retrieval augmentation in medical AI applications.

stories.title: AnyMo: Geometry-Aware Setup-Agnostic Modeling of Human Motion in the Wild
stories.author: Flora Salim
bullet_points: The research addresses the major challenge of setup dependence in human motion sensing using wearable Inertial Measurement Units (IMUs).
The proposed method, AnyMo, aims to create motion representations that are robust and transferable across different sensing setups and devices.
This work is relevant to the broader application of wearable technology for continuous, real-world human motion tracking.

stories.title: AMEL: Accumulated Message Effects on LLM Judgments
stories.author: Sid-ali Temkit
bullet_points: The research investigates the 'Accumulated Message Effect' (AMEL), where prior conversation history biases subsequent judgments made by LLMs.
The study tested this bias across 11 different LLMs from major providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) and open-source models.
The findings address a critical issue in using LLMs as automated evaluators (e.g., code review, content moderation) in multi-turn conversations.

stories.title: Memory has grown to nearly two-thirds of AI chip component costs
stories.author: intelkishan
bullet_points: Memory components are becoming a dominant cost driver in AI chips.
The cost of memory now accounts for nearly two-thirds of the total AI chip component expenses.
This highlights a critical area of focus for future AI hardware optimization and cost reduction.

stories.title: DeepSeek reasonix, DeepSeek native coding agent with high caching and low cost
stories.author: Alifatisk
bullet_points: Introduction of DeepSeek reasonix, a native coding agent.
Key features highlighted are high caching capabilities and low operational cost.
The announcement suggests advancements in efficient AI coding tools.

stories.title: Flick (YC F25) Is Hiring Front End Engineer to Build Figma for AI Filmmaking
stories.author: rayruiwang
bullet_points: Flick, a YC-backed startup, is hiring a Front End Engineer.
The company's goal is to build a tool described as 'Figma for AI Filmmaking'.
This indicates a trend toward specialized, AI-driven creative tools in the media industry.

stories.title: FinePrint — An AI Pocket Lawyer That Decodes Predatory Contracts Using Gemma 4
stories.author: dj29
bullet_points: FinePrint is an AI application designed to analyze and decode predatory contracts.
The project utilizes Gemma 4, indicating a focus on leveraging large language models for legal tech.
It was developed as a submission for the Gemma 4 Challenge, highlighting community-driven AI innovation.
